key_facts: README teaches the generated workflow sequence `constitution`, `specify`, `plan`, `tasks`, `implement`, `auto`, with `specify -> plan` as mainline and `specify -> deep-research -> plan` as optional feasibility branch.
README names `map-scan` followed by `map-build` as the required brownfield gate before deeper existing-code work.
README describes `test` as a compatibility router to `test-scan` or `test-build` and records the durable testing artifacts produced by those workflows.
`.planning/STATE.md` says v1.3 is archived, there is no active phase or milestone plan, and the next planning step is to start a new milestone.

`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-map-scan-build-implementation.md` records the intended breaking change from one-step map-codebase to two-step map-scan/map-build and lists the modified shared surfaces.
Extension and preset docs/catalogs are product surfaces for optional add-ons, not generated cache.
recommended_atlas_updates: Document `.planning/*` as historical/current product planning state, with v1.3 complete and no active milestone.
Document map-scan/map-build implementation history as recent structural change.
Document extension and preset surfaces in operations/integrations.
unknowns: Older planning artifacts are historical unless current `.planning/STATE.md` reactivates them.
